{"author": null, "closed_at": null, "collaborating_teams": null, "collaborating_users": null, "created_at": null, "credits": [{"login": "alcls01111", "type": "reporter"}], "credits_detailed": [{"state": "accepted", "type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/57210489?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alcls01111/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alcls01111/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alcls01111/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alcls01111/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/alcls01111", "id": 57210489, "login": "alcls01111", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjU3MjEwNDg5", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alcls01111/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alcls01111/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alcls01111/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alcls01111/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/alcls01111/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/alcls01111", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": null, "cvss": {"score": 5.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 5.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:L/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:N/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": null, "vector_string": null}}, "cwe_ids": ["CWE-276"], "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-276", "name": "Incorrect Default Permissions"}], "description": "### Impact

When running `nuxt dev` on Linux (Node.js 20+, outside Docker / StackBlitz), Nuxt's internal vite-node IPC server binds to a Linux abstract-namespace Unix socket (`\0nuxt-vite-node-<pid>-<ts>.sock`). Abstract sockets have no filesystem inode and therefore no permission bits: any local UID on the host that can read `/proc/net/unix` can enumerate the socket and connect to it.

The IPC server does not perform any peer-credential or shared-secret check before dispatching requests. The `module` request type passes its `moduleId` field straight into Vite's SSR `fetchModule()`, which is not gated by Vite's HTTP-layer `server.fs.allow` deny-list. A co-resident unprivileged local user can therefore request paths like `/home/<dev>/project/.env?raw` or `~/.ssh/id_rsa?raw` and read the developer's secrets through the dev server's SSR plugin pipeline. The `resolve` request type additionally enables filesystem probing.

This affects developers running `nuxt dev` on shared multi-tenant Linux hosts (lab machines, shared bastions, CI runners shared between jobs without per-job container isolation). It does not affect:

- Production builds (`nuxt build` / `nuxt start`). The IPC server only runs in development.
- macOS or Windows developers.
- Docker / StackBlitz environments, which already fall back to a filesystem socket.
- Single-user laptops or per-job containerised CI.

### Patches

Fixed in `nuxt@4.4.7` (commit [`1f9f4767`](https://github.com/nuxt/nuxt/commit/1f9f4767a8725104da9bee872bb8d35246f25ae5)) and backported to `nuxt@3.21.7` (commit [`c293bf95`](https://github.com/nuxt/nuxt/commit/c293bf9503ccb3bc9559bff4a1f592f99063c9ea)).

The fix removes the abstract-namespace branch entirely. The IPC server now always binds to a filesystem Unix socket under the OS temp directory and explicitly `chmod 0600`s it after `listen()`, restricting connections to the owning UID. If the chmod fails for any reason, the server closes rather than serve requests on an unrestricted channel.

### Workarounds

If you cannot upgrade immediately on an affected host:

- Run `nuxt dev` inside a container or VM with no other tenants. Docker already triggers the filesystem-socket fallback in vulnerable versions and that fallback is unaffected.
- Bind the dev process to a single-user namespace (`unshare -U`, rootless containers).
- Restrict `/proc/net/unix` visibility via `hidepid=2` mount options where applicable, though this is partial mitigation only.

### References

- Affected file: `packages/vite/src/plugins/vite-node.ts`
- CWE-276: Incorrect Default Permissions

### Credit

Reported by Anthropic / Claude as part of Anthropic's coordinated vulnerability disclosure programme, reference ANT-2026-MSNKZFAT. Thanks to the Anthropic security team for the report and the detailed reproduction.

Independently reported by [@alcls01111](https://github.com/alcls01111) via GitHub's coordinated disclosure flow (`GHSA-5gvc-46gq-948j`), closed as a duplicate of this advisory.
", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-534h-c3cw-v3h9", "html_url": "https://github.com/nuxt/nuxt/security/advisories/GHSA-534h-c3cw-v3h9", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-534h-c3cw-v3h9"}], "private_fork": null, "published_at": "2026-06-02T14:32:16Z", "publisher": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/28706372?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/danielroe/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/danielroe/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/danielroe/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/danielroe/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/danielroe", "id": 28706372, "login": "danielroe", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjI4NzA2Mzcy", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/danielroe/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/danielroe/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/danielroe/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/danielroe/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/danielroe/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/danielroe", "user_view_type": "public"}, "severity": "medium", "state": "published", "submission": null, "summary": "Nuxt dev server vite-node IPC socket is world-connectable on Linux", "updated_at": "2026-06-02T14:32:16Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/repos/nuxt/nuxt/security-advisories/GHSA-534h-c3cw-v3h9", "vulnerabilities": [{"package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "nuxt"}, "patched_versions": "4.4.7", "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 4.0.0, < 4.4.7"}, {"package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "nuxt"}, "patched_versions": "3.21.7", "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": ">= 3.18.0, < 3.21.7"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: Nuxt dev server vite-node IPC socket is world-connectable on Linux
